content.decision_question: Should reform proceed incrementally by removing only the earliest selection point, accepting risk of demand displacement, or should it address all selection points simultaneously to avoid displacement at the cost of greater political conflict?
content.evidence_boundary: Evidence on displacement effects from partial detracking in other systems could help estimate the likely magnitude of demand relocation to age-12, but no comparable partial-reform precedent exists in the current record, so the actual displacement-versus-reduction balance for Hungary specifically remains unresolved by existing evidence.
content.tension: Partial reform (capping only age-10 entry) offers a lower-conflict, more politically survivable path, but risks merely displacing selection pressure to the remaining age-12 entry point without net segregation reduction, while full reform addresses the funnel comprehensively but faces much higher resistance.
content.title: Incremental versus comprehensive reform scope
